title: Agent definition and Quality Manual cleanup (post-CR-112 retrospective)

comment:
Pre-review of CR-113 v0.2 (IN_PRE_REVIEW). Frontmatter verified: title='Agent definition and Quality Manual cleanup (post-CR-112 retrospective)', revision_summary references v0.2 §5.8 addition. Structure follows CR template (sections 1-12 present). Spot-checked current-state claims against the live tree: DS references confirmed in all four TU agent files; 'one-way bridge' confirmed in tu_sketch.md:97; '(implied by rejection logic)' confirmed in 03-Workflows.md:46; 'Inbox Notifications' confirmed in all five named agent files. The substantive scope and structure changes are well-grounded in the retrospective.

Findings requiring resolution before pre-approval:

1. SCOPE OMISSION (substantive): §5.8 'Invocation Modes' is scoped to five agent files (qa, tu_ui, tu_sketch, tu_scene, tu_sim). However, bu.md also contains the same '## Inbox Notifications' section (verified at bu.md:103). The §5.8 justification — that 'an agent reading its own definition file under the Task tool sees only container-mode guidance' — applies equally to bu, which has been used as a reviewer in prior CRs. The CR does not explain whether bu is intentionally out of scope or inadvertently omitted. Resolution: either (a) add bu.md to the §5.8 scope and update §2.3, §7.1, EI-5, and the 'five agent definition files' phrasing accordingly, or (b) state explicitly in §2.3 / §7.3 'Out of scope' why bu is excluded. The current 'five files' count is inconsistent with the project's actual six-agent set without explanation.

2. UNBOUNDED EXECUTION ITEM (scope integrity): §5.4 instructs the executor, regarding the executable-documents POST_REVIEWED row in routing-quickref.md, to 'verify it is accurate; if not, align it with the same wording.' This is a conditional edit with no corresponding EI. Per QMS-Policy.md §6, execution must deliver the pre-approved scope and only that scope; an open-ended verify-and-maybe-edit instruction without a dedicated EI defeats the pre-review of that work. Resolution: either (a) verify the POST_REVIEWED row pre-approval and either delete the conditional or convert it to a definite edit captured in a new EI, or (b) drop the conditional from §5.4 entirely if no edit is intended.

3. SECTION 7.2 STRUCTURE: The CR template specifies §7.2 'Documents Affected' as a table with columns Document / Change Type / Description. CR-113's §7.2 is a narrative paragraph instead. Resolution: convert §7.2 to the table form (or, if no controlled documents are affected beyond those already in §7.1, state 'None — see §7.1' explicitly).

4. EI-6 SCOPE PHRASING (minor): EI-6 says 'add the REVIEWED → checkout → DRAFT row per §5.4' but §5.4 covers three files; EI-6 actually covers only routing-quickref.md (03-Workflows.md is EI-7, review-guide.md is EI-8). Resolution: tighten EI-6 to 'per §5.4 (routing-quickref.md non-executable status table row)' for clarity.

5. §5.7 LITERAL BLOCK (minor): The §5.7 example block ends with '(Existing content follows here unchanged.)' which is meta-instruction, not literal content to insert. This is interpretable from context but ambiguous in form. Resolution: move the 'preserve existing content' note out of the code fence and into prose.

Findings 1 and 2 are substantive (scope integrity). Findings 3-5 are structural/formatting. All five must be addressed before pre-approval per the zero-tolerance standard.

Pre-review (v0.3) — REQUEST UPDATES.

Verification of prior round's five findings: ALL ADDRESSED.
1. (Pass) §5.8 scope is now all six agent files — verified line 295 lists 'qa.md, bu.md, tu_ui.md, tu_sketch.md, tu_scene.md, tu_sim.md'.
2. (Pass) §5.4 routing-quickref edits made definite — 'Add a row immediately after' (§5.4.1) and 'Replace this row's third column' are unconditional.
3. (Pass) §7.2 is now in table form (lines 372-378).
4. (Pass) §5.4 restructured into explicit §§5.4.1/5.4.2/5.4.3 (lines 167, 193, 207); EI-6/EI-7/EI-8 reference these subsections.
5. (Pass) §5.7 contains the literal verbatim subsection text (lines 270-287), not a meta-instruction.

NEW FINDING (one) — internal consistency defect between §2.3 and §7.1:

§2.3 'Files Affected' (line 45) correctly lists '.claude/agents/bu.md — replace Inbox Notifications with Invocation Modes' as one of SIX agent files affected.

§7.1 'Files Affected' table (lines 359-363) lists only FIVE agent files: tu_ui, tu_sketch, tu_scene, tu_sim, qa. The .claude/agents/bu.md row is MISSING from the §7.1 table.

This is the same scope omission that the prior cycle's Finding 1 corrected in §5.8 — now §7.1 needs the same correction. EI-5 (line 476) and §5.8 both correctly call for editing all six files; only the §7.1 table undercounts.

Required fix: Add a row to the §7.1 table:
| .claude/agents/bu.md | Modify | Replace Inbox Notifications with Invocation Modes |

Once §7.1 lists all six agent files consistent with §2.3, §5.8, and EI-5, the document will be ready to advance.

Frontmatter: title='Agent definition and Quality Manual cleanup (post-CR-112 retrospective)' — present, non-empty. revision_summary substantively describes the v0.3 changes. No other deficiencies found.

Cycle-3 pre-review (v0.4): COMPLIANT. Cycle-2 finding resolved —  row now present in the §7.1 Files Affected table (line 360), aligning §7.1 with §2.3 (line 41), §5.8 (line 291), and EI-5 (line 473). Frontmatter verified: title='Agent definition and Quality Manual cleanup (post-CR-112 retrospective)'; revision_summary describes the v0.4 fix and retains v0.3 history. Document structure matches the CR template for a document-only CR (§§1-12 present in order; §7.4, §7.5, and code-CR §9 phases correctly omitted per type guidance). Internal consistency verified: §2.3 lists 11 files; §7.1 lists 11 files; §5 has eight subsections (5.1-5.8) covering all 11 files; §9 phases map to §5 subsections; EI-1 through EI-13 trace to §9 phases. No author-time  placeholders; only execution-time  placeholders remain (correct per CR template Placeholder Convention). No TBD/TODO/FIXME. §5.3 reviewer-assignment table is mutually exclusive and collectively exhaustive over  (process_objects.py and simulation_geometry.py explicitly carved out of tu_sketch into tu_scene and tu_sim respectively). References §12 valid and well-formed. No new findings.
